WV.label: Without Vision
WV.source_note: AFB: ~3.4 million Americans with severe visual impairment or blindness

LV.label: Limited Vision
LV.source_note: ACS 2023 + supplemental estimates: vision difficulty (all severity levels); ~2.4% of U.S. population

WPC.label: Without Perception of Color
WPC.source_note: NIH/NEI: ~8% of males and ~0.5% of females have color vision deficiency

WH.label: Without Hearing
WH.source_note: NIDCD: ~1.1 million Americans with functional deafness

LH.label: Limited Hearing
LH.source_note: NIDCD/ACS combined hearing difficulty estimates: ~3.5% of U.S. population

WS.label: Without Speech
WS.source_note: NIDCD estimate: severe non-verbal or speech-absent population

LM.label: Limited Manipulation
LM.source_note: ACS 2023 self-care difficulty + dexterity estimates: ~2.2% of U.S. population

LRS.label: Limited Reach and Strength
LRS.source_note: ACS 2023: ambulatory difficulty; ~5.8% of U.S. population

LLCLA.label: Limited Language, Cognitive, and Learning Abilities
LLCLA.source_note: ACS 2023/CDC combined cognitive and learning disability estimates: ~4.7% of U.S. population

fpc_exclusion.census_source: U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2023 1-Year Estimates, Table B18101

axe_findings.title: Buttons do not have an accessible name
axe_findings.description: When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. [Learn how to make buttons more accessible](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/button-name).

  Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  Element does not have an explicit <label>
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

axe_findings.title: `button`, `link`, and `menuitem` elements do not have accessible names.
axe_findings.description: When an element doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. [Learn how to make command elements more accessible](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/aria-command-name).

items.explanation:
Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute

axe_findings.title: Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0
axe_findings.description: A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. [Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/tabindex).

items.snippet: <a href="#main-content" class="btn btn-default margin-top-3" tabindex="1" data-once="download-role-button lang-fix-multilingual">

Fix any of the following:
  Element has a tabindex greater than 0

top_urls.organization_name: Department of Health and Human Services

axe_findings.title: `<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute
axe_findings.description: If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. [Learn more about the `lang` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/html-has-lang).

items.explanation:
Fix any of the following:
  The <html> element does not have a lang attribute

axe_findings.title: Document does not have a main landmark.
axe_findings.description: One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. [Learn more about landmarks](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/landmark-one-main).

items.explanation:
Fix all of the following:
  Document does not have a main landmark

top_urls.organization_name: Department of Health and Human Services

axe_findings.title: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
axe_findings.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

items.explanation:
Fix any of the following:
  Element has insufficient color contrast of 3.5 (foreground color: #ff0000, background color: #f0f0f0, font size: 10.5pt (14px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

axe_findings.title: Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order
axe_findings.description: Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. [Learn more about heading order](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/heading-order).

items.explanation:
Fix any of the following:
  Heading order invalid

axe_findings.title: Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes
axe_findings.description: Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. [Learn more about the `alt` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/image-alt).

items.explanation:
Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have an alt attribute
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

axe_findings.title: Form elements do not have associated labels
axe_findings.description: Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. [Learn more about form element labels](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/label).

items.explanation:
Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  Element does not have an explicit <label>
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element has no placeholder attribute
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

  Element has no title attribute
  Element has no placeholder attribute
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

axe_findings.title: Links do not have a discernible name
axe_findings.description: Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. [Learn how to make links accessible](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/link-name).

axe_findings.title: `[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles
axe_findings.description: Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. [Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/aria-allowed-attr).

items.snippet: <div class="toolExpanderHeader mdtext flexSpread" aria-controls="histogramContent" aria-expanded="false" tabindex="0">
items.explanation:
Fix all of the following:
  ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"

axe_findings.title: `[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents
axe_findings.description: Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. [Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/aria-hidden-focus).

items.snippet: <calcite-icon id="dockRight" color="black" tab-index="50" tabindex="50" style="margin-left: 1rem; color: black; cursor: pointer;" aria-hidden="true" scale="m" icon="dock-right">
items.explanation:
Fix all of the following:
  Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM

axe_findings.title: Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order
axe_findings.description: Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. [Learn more about heading order](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/heading-order).

axe_findings.title: Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0
axe_findings.description: A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. [Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/tabindex).

axe_findings.title: Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes

axe_findings.description: Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. [Learn more about form element labels](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/label).
